rows.locks: The M93 Unity demo fixture parses Force-Text .unity/.prefab/.asset plus .meta GUID references into Rust-owned IR with scenes, prefabs, assets, inputs, fidelity records, missing oracle records, and zero claimed ported units.

Unity behavior-bearing MonoBehaviour data, prefab override caveats, physics re-simulation, and unsupported gaps remain Yellow/Red fidelity findings; validation rejects attempts to launder these gaps into a clean report.

The Unity adapter validators reject auto-translated claimed_ported_units and oracle-missing records with port permission, so no imported Unity unit is treated as ported without captured passing oracle evidence.
Unity adapter state hashes are stable for identical canonical Force-Text inputs, change when source data changes, and validation rejects stale hashes after IR tampering.

Coverage v81 records the Unity Force-Text source-only legal boundary, one-way on-ramp, clean-room Era R re-derivation hand-off, Rust data-plane ownership, no Studio trusted writes, no decompiled source copying, and #1/#23 open governance anchors.

boundary: Scenario Coverage v81 is a Rust data-plane regression suite for Era O M93 Unity 2D adapter to IR. It locks source-project/open-text Unity Force-Text skeleton import, .meta GUID reference resolution, no Unity runtime bridge, no auto-port or ported claim without a passing oracle, honest Yellow/Red fidelity gaps for lossy imports, deterministic state-hash behavior including stale-hash rejection, and Rust-owned artifact truth. Elixir/Phoenix Studio is not touched and has no trusted write or artifact semantics authority. #1 and #23 remain open.
